{{Expand Japanese|詔勅|date=November 2025}}
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:詔勅]]


The term '''Imperial Edict''' (詔勅, *shōchoku*) in Yamato language is called "mikotonori," meaning to declare (*noru*) the emperor’s words (*mikoto*). After the '''Meiji Restoration''', imperial edicts were formally recognized as pronouncements (*rinkon*) issued through the emperor’s words.

During the '''Shōwa era''', particularly during the Pacific War, imperial edicts broadly encompassed all '''imperial directives''' (*chokushi*). The term refers collectively to '''imperial decrees''' (*shōsho*), '''imperial rescripts''' (*chokushō*), and '''imperial proclamations''' (*chokugo*).

In constitutional studies before World War II, imperial edicts were defined as the direct '''imperial will''' expressed to the public. Among the various forms in which the emperor’s '''sovereign prerogative''' was exercised, '''imperial edicts were regarded as the most significant'''. Written imperial edicts bore the '''imperial signature''' (*shinsyo*) and were affixed with either the '''Imperial Seal''' (*Gyoji*) or the '''State Seal''' (*Kokuji*). There were also '''oral imperial edicts''', known as '''imperial proclamations''' (*chokugo*).


== See Also ==
{{translated page|ja|詔勅|version=99991506|comment=Imported full ja history}}



[[ja:詔勅]]
[[ja:宣命]]
[[ja:位記]]

<!--Imperial decrees--><!--Imperial decrees--><!--Imperial decrees--><!--Imperial decrees--><!--Imperial decrees--><!--Imperial decrees--><!--Imperial decrees--><!--Imperial decrees--><!--Imperial decrees-->

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{wikidata link|Q67421629}}
[[Category:Japanese Imperial rescripts]]
[[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
